calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-14-2015 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Punitive Damage Alegations From Plaintiffs Complaint
Off Calendar Jul-16-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Michael Corbett, of Law Offices of Nikolaus W. Reed, Tel. 925.876.5282, for plaintiffs;
Marilou R. Bustonera, Gilsleider & McMahon, Tel. 925.446.3116, for defendants, appearing via CourtCall.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS DENNIS' CHAMPION AUTO BODY & REPAIR, DENNIS KIM AND KIMBERLY KIM'S Motion To Strike Punitive Damage Alegations From Plaintiff's Complaint is denied. Under CCP 436(a) to prevail on a motion to strike, the movant must establish that the complaint contains irrelevant, false or improper allegations. A motion to strike punitive damages allegations may be granted where the claim sued upon would not support an award of punitive damages as a matter of law [See Civ.C. 3294(a); Commodore Home Systems, Inc. v. Sup.Ct. (Brown) (1982) 32 C3d 211, 214-215.] Here, (1) Defendant has not established the complaint contains irrelevant, false or improper allegations and, (2) the allegations contained in the complaint may support punitive damages as a matter of law. Order signed in open court. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302. (302/EHG)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-15-2015 Continued To Sep-02-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
